name: Sun
subtitle: The star at the center of our Solar System
summary: A massive sphere of hot plasma that provides nearly all the energy for the Solar System.
description: The Sun contains more than 99 percent of the Solar System's mass. Its gravity holds the planets, moons, asteroids, and comets in orbit, while its light and heat make life on Earth possible.

name: Mercury
subtitle: Smallest planet and closest to the Sun
summary: A rocky world with extreme temperature swings and a heavily cratered surface.
description: Mercury orbits closest to the Sun and completes a year in only 88 Earth days. With almost no atmosphere to retain heat, its surface shifts from scorching daytime temperatures to freezing nights.

name: Venus
subtitle: Earth-sized planet with a dense atmosphere
summary: A bright rocky planet wrapped in thick clouds and intense greenhouse heat.
description: Venus is similar to Earth in size, but its dense carbon dioxide atmosphere traps heat so efficiently that it has the hottest surface of any planet in the Solar System.

name: Earth
subtitle: Our ocean world
summary: The only known world with life, liquid surface water, and a breathable atmosphere.
description: Earth is a rocky planet with oceans, active geology, and a protective atmosphere. Its Moon stabilizes its axial tilt and shapes the tides.

name: Moon
subtitle: Earth's natural satellite
summary: A rocky companion that shapes Earth's tides and lights the night sky.
description: The Moon is Earth's only natural satellite. Its surface preserves impact history, and its gravity creates ocean tides that have influenced Earth for billions of years.

name: Terra
subtitle: NASA Earth-observing satellite
summary: A flagship Earth science satellite that has monitored climate, atmosphere, land, and oceans since 1999.
description: Terra is part of NASA's Earth Observing System. Its instruments collect data on clouds, radiation, land cover, ocean color, and atmospheric conditions, helping scientists study Earth's changing climate.

name: Aqua
subtitle: NASA water-cycle observation satellite
summary: An Earth science satellite focused on water in the atmosphere, oceans, land, ice, and snow.
description: Aqua is part of NASA's Earth Observing System. Its instruments study the planet's water cycle, including evaporation, clouds, precipitation, sea ice, and surface temperatures.

name: TESS
subtitle: Transiting Exoplanet Survey Satellite
summary: A NASA space telescope that searches nearby stars for planets crossing in front of them.
description: TESS monitors large areas of the sky to find exoplanets using the transit method. Its wide-field cameras look for tiny dips in starlight caused by planets passing between their stars and Earth.

name: TDRS
subtitle: Tracking and Data Relay Satellite
summary: A communications satellite type used to relay data between spacecraft and ground stations.
description: NASA's Tracking and Data Relay Satellites support communications with spacecraft in Earth orbit. They help missions send science data, telemetry, and voice communications back to Earth.

name: Apollo Lunar Module
subtitle: Crewed Moon landing spacecraft
summary: The two-stage spacecraft that carried Apollo astronauts from lunar orbit down to the Moon's surface and back.
description: The Apollo Lunar Module was built to operate only in space. Its descent stage landed on the Moon, while the ascent stage lifted crews back to the command module in lunar orbit.

name: Saturn V
subtitle: Apollo-era Moon rocket
summary: The powerful launch vehicle that sent Apollo crews toward the Moon.
description: Saturn V remains one of the most capable rockets ever flown. Its three stages launched Apollo spacecraft from Earth and placed crews on a trajectory to the Moon.

name: Astronaut
subtitle: Human space explorer
summary: A suited astronaut model representing the people who work beyond Earth.
description: Astronauts train for spacecraft operations, science, spacewalks, and emergency procedures. Their suits provide pressure, oxygen, cooling, and protection in hostile environments.

name: International Space Station
subtitle: Crewed orbital laboratory
summary: A modular research station where crews conduct science in low Earth orbit.
description: The International Space Station is a partnership laboratory assembled in orbit. It supports long-duration human spaceflight, microgravity research, Earth observation, and technology demonstrations.

name: Hubble Space Telescope
subtitle: Orbital observatory
summary: A space telescope that has transformed astronomy with deep views of the universe.
description: Hubble observes above Earth's atmosphere, giving it a clear view in visible, ultraviolet, and near-infrared light. Its images and measurements have shaped modern understanding of stars, galaxies, and cosmic expansion.

name: Space Shuttle
subtitle: Reusable orbital spacecraft
summary: NASA's winged spacecraft for crew, cargo, satellites, and station assembly missions.
description: The Space Shuttle launched like a rocket and landed like an aircraft. It carried astronauts, satellites, laboratories, and major International Space Station components to low Earth orbit.

name: Mars
subtitle: The red planet
summary: A cold desert world with polar caps, canyons, volcanoes, and signs of ancient water.
description: Mars is a rocky planet with a thin atmosphere and iron-rich dust that gives it its red color. Ancient river valleys and minerals suggest liquid water once flowed on its surface.

name: Jupiter
subtitle: Largest planet in the Solar System
summary: A giant world of clouds, storms, strong magnetism, and many moons.
description: Jupiter is a gas giant with more mass than all other planets combined. Its Great Red Spot is a long-lived storm larger than Earth.

name: Saturn
subtitle: Ringed gas giant
summary: A low-density giant planet surrounded by bright icy rings.
description: Saturn is best known for its broad ring system, made of countless icy particles. It has a complex family of moons, including Titan with its dense atmosphere.

name: Uranus
subtitle: Ice giant tilted on its side
summary: A pale blue ice giant with an extreme axial tilt and faint rings.
description: Uranus rotates with its axis tilted almost sideways compared with its orbit. Methane in its atmosphere absorbs red light, giving the planet its blue-green color.

name: Neptune
subtitle: Distant blue ice giant
summary: A windy outer planet with deep blue clouds and faint rings.
description: Neptune is the farthest major planet from the Sun. It has extremely fast winds, dark storm systems, and a family of moons led by Triton.
